name: Catfish
description: Farmed in freshwater ponds and rivers, this fish is a staple in local cuisine, especially for making adobo dishes.

name: Sea Grapes
description: This edible green algae, often used in salads, grows in the shallow waters of coastal areas.

name: Red Snapper
description: Found in deep coral reefs, red snapper is prized for its firm flesh and delicate flavor.

name: Snapper
description: Various species of snappers are abundant in the coral reefs and deeper offshore waters.

name: Spanner Crab
description: Known for its spiny shell and deep-sea habitat, curacha is highly prized for its sweet, tender meat.

name: Mantis Shrimp
description: A crustacean known for its powerful claws and vibrant colors, found in the coastal areas of Northern Mindanao.

name: Goby
description: Small, colorful fish often found in rocky tidal pools and reef areas.

name: Tabios
description: The world’s smallest commercially harvested fish, endemic to small lakes and shallow water regions.

name: Sardine
description: A small, oily fish found in schools, often used for canning or drying.

name: Mackerel Tuna
description: Commonly found in the deeper waters of the region, it is an important species for both commercial and subsistence fishing.
